{"step_by_step_thinking": "Velocardial facial syndrome, also known as DiGeorge syndrome, is caused by a deletion in chromosome 22, specifically at the 22q11.2 locus, as confirmed by multiple sources. Document [14] confirms that DiGeorge syndrome is caused by a microdeletion of chromosome 22 at the 22q11.2 locus. The query incorrectly states that the deletion occurs in chromosome 21, which is false. Trisomy 21 (Down syndrome) is a different condition involving an extra chromosome 21, not a deletion causing DiGeorge or velocardiofacial syndrome.
